## [00:00 - 03:02]

So, strategic partnerships is a prospecting method that's going to allow you to cut your teeth on some pretty big brands or clients. It is a longer term place strategy and sometimes it can take up to six months to a year to really start to get traction from what you're doing. And also, it does require a lot of nurturing and some agreements in place with whatever strategic partner you have. In essence, here's how it works. Basically you collaborate or you create a partnership with someone in the marketplace that shares a similar audience to you or the same audience, I should say, to you. And they will then send you leads. Now, why would they do that? Well, there's a lot of reasons. Sometimes the clients that they're working with will have a desire or a need for the services that you offer and so they will refer them on to you. Now, when you start to get into the agency space, a lot of the strategic partnerships that you look to found will be with other agencies. So if you're a Facebook media buyer, you could align yourself with PR companies, for example, PR agencies because a lot of those don't offer Facebook advertising services but their clients are asking for it. At the freelancing stage, a tip that I would start with is actually almost looking at the types of businesses that are serving or servicing the exact same clientele that you're trying to go after. So here's an example. If you're trying to go after grocery store owners, if that's your niche or your demographic, then you want to look at what kind of businesses service that exact audience. So are there lawyers out there that specialize in this niche? Are there accountants out there that do that? Are there suppliers like software tools or POD systems that work with this type of audience? Now the easiest way in for this type of strategy is to identify those suppliers or those businesses or merchants that are working with the exact same audience, your ideal customer essentially. Notice them all out and once you do, start to broker a relationship with that individual. So let's pretend that you're going after a lawyer. What you would essentially do is reach out, introduce yourself and there's some templates below this video that you can tap into and use for this, but reach out to this lawyer and broker a relationship with them. Now what I would recommend here is that you actually offer this person your services pro bono in exchange for getting referrals or leads for them. So let's say your Facebook media buyer essentially going to a lawyer and saying to them, hey,

## [03:02 - 06:02]

I will run your campaigns for you at no cost. I'll help you get more of these clients. I also happen to work with these clients and if you like what I do and you have success with what I do, then if you want to recommend me to your leads or the clients that you work with, then that's great. That's all I ask for. So essentially there's no stipulation that they have to do that and there's very little risk for them at that stage. You're giving them something of high value and in exchange you're getting the opportunity to create a pipeline for yourself with really high quality leads because if you think about it, those are all going to be referrals. And many of those are going to be the perfect kind of clients for you. So that's essentially a strategic partnership in an overview. Now there's different ways to structure this. If you don't want to give the service pro bono to whatever strategic partner you have, one option you have is to offer a kickback. So a kickback would be a sum of money that you would provide that strategic partner for sending you a referral. When you get into bigger figures like in the agency space, that's usually around $2,000. So if I send you a client and you close that client, you will give me $2,000 for that. This gives me a really big incentive to keep sending you clients at this stage and for you, it works out just as well because you would probably pay around that price anyways to acquire a customer. When you're freelancing, those numbers are going to be a bit smaller, more around the sort of $500 mark. You still want it to be juicy enough that it gives them that incentive to send you leads, right? So don't get too scrimpy on that. The other option that you have is look at connectors within your industry. So who are the people that are really well connected within the ideal target audience that you have? Sometimes those could be people that are running events for that audience. Those could be people that are in the same masterminds as your ideal customer avatar. These are connectors that have lots and lots of connections with your ideal target audience and you approach it exactly the same way. Hey, let me help you with your website or your media buying or whatever, your graphic design, your content, whatever it is that you're offering. And if you like what I do and you get results, then I'd love it if you could also recommend me. By the way, you don't even need to tell them that. Reciprocity is a really powerful thing in human psychology. By the very nature of you offering services for free, people will feel very indebted to you and will want to connect you as long as you're getting them great results and they're happy with it. They will want to send you business because they're going to have this really awkward feeling inside of I'm getting this free service and I need to balance this out a bit. So this is essentially the concept of strategic partnerships.

## [06:02 - 06:38]

The important thing here is that you clearly identify who those potential strategic partners could be for you. So really nail that down and then make sure that you're not just approaching this as like this is going to be something by next week. It's a longer term play. You're building a relationship with someone and you don't want to approach them too aggressively. Even just offering your services right off the bat can be a bit too aggressive. So broker the relationship first. Go in a bit softly and then from there you will see things. The momentum will start to build. The windmill will start to turn.
